CORRECTION to the 08:12 DAILY_PLAN entry: today 2026-07-03 IS a long-form publish day. The 08:12 review concluded 'no long-form exists to publish' by reading only main's tracking (NEXT_VIDEOS.md LONG-2=PENDING/TBD, weekly_plans/2026-27.md LONG-2=TBD). That was incomplete: open PR #330 (branch claude/kind-franklin-8tzmrx, 'long-form-pipeline: LONG-2 (v5-flannan) produced + scheduled Fri 2026-07-03 17:00 UTC') shows LONG-2 = The Flannan Isles Lighthouse Mystery (1900), run_id v5-flannan, yt_id mgdNSwtkrnw, scheduled on the channel for 2026-07-03T17:00:00Z (verified via Studio read-back at production time per that PR). The YouTube schedule is set on the channel independently of the repo, so Flannan WILL go live today 17:00 UTC regardless of merge state. ROOT DRIFT: PR #330 is an unmerged draft, so main's source-of-truth files never received the LONG-2 metadata -> the manager routine (which reads main) is blind to a long-form publishing in ~9h. Same pattern strands the whole routine-PR flow: #326/#330/#334 are all open unmerged drafts (my own #338 auto-merged fine via auto-merge-claude.yml, so the workflow works for fresh PRs; the older ones are stuck — likely base-behind-main / conflicts). DOWNSTREAM: today's community-tab plan row (Fri 18:00 'Roanoke ANSWER drop (if shipped)') is also stale vs the actual Flannan publish. NOT auto-merging #330: production PR merges are the owner's call, and the same routine-PR flow has an open security flag (#326/#334: mcp_stealth.py direct-HTTP bypass of the MCP classifier) awaiting owner review. Could not independently re-verify the live Studio schedule this run (would need camoufox; oEmbed returns nothing for a still-scheduled/private video). ACTION: owner to decide whether to merge PR #330 so main reflects the shipped Flannan long-form, and to reconcile today's community-tab post to Flannan rather than the stale Roanoke row.

w27_discovery_flight19 — standalone 55s vertical Short on Flight 19 (Dec 5 1945). Script: 111-word VO written fresh (script-smith), calm/evidence-aware per community-manager voice rules. Voice: David Documentary (ppLqTilh7rH7fbUVlXsf), eleven_multilingual_v2, generated via direct ElevenLabs REST call using ELEVENLABS_KEY (the mcp__mcphub__elevenlabs-text_to_speech MCP tool saved its output to the MCP server's own /root/Desktop, unreachable from this container — same cross-host filesystem-isolation class as BLOCKER_2026-06-30/2026-07-02; direct API call avoided the bridge entirely). Assets: 3 Wikimedia Commons TBM/TBF Avenger photos via fetch_wikimedia_assets.py (formation shot doubles as thumb base). Render: fresh 1080x1920 Ken-Burns build (3 images, xfade crossfades, muxed with narration) since this is a standalone discovery Short, not a long-form crop. Cut: shorts/short_cutter_v2.py with hook/outro cards — added a 'source_is_portrait' config option (skips the landscape-crop assumption for freshly-rendered portrait sources) and a 'hook_fontsize' override (default 220 overflowed the 4-line hook text; used 150), both additive/backward-compatible. Thumb: shorts/make_fern_thumb.py — fixed a broken hardcoded font path (pointed at a different host's filesystem, OSError in this container) to use an available system Liberation Sans Bold. NOT uploaded: no working camoufox-stealth MCP tool available in this session/sandbox to reach YouTube Studio. mp4 (19MB) and thumbnail are local-only production artifacts, not committed to git per repo convention (no binary renders in git history) — state, config, script text, and metadata committed instead. See LEARNINGS.md BLOCKER_2026-07-04 for manual-recovery / next-session instructions.
